programs.name: Oklahoma Medicaid SoonerCare
programs.what_it_does: Provides health coverage to eligible low-income seniors and disabled individuals, including doctor visits, hospital care, and long-term services. In Oklahoma, it is administered as SoonerCare with specific eligibility for aged, blind, and disabled (ABD) categories.
programs.who_its_for: Seniors aged 65+ or disabled individuals with low income and assets meeting state Medicaid thresholds.
programs.benefit_value: Varies by eligibility; covers medical costs up to full scope for qualified seniors.

programs.name: Home and Community-Based Services (HCBS) Waivers (ADvantage Waiver)
programs.what_it_does: Offers in-home services like personal care and homemaker assistance to prevent nursing home placement. Administered through Oklahoma's ADvantage Waiver program under Title III and Medicaid.
programs.who_its_for: Seniors 65+ needing nursing home level care but able to remain safely at home.
programs.benefit_value: Personal care hours, adult day care, and other services tailored to individual needs.

programs.name: Programs of All-Inclusive Care for the Elderly (PACE)
programs.what_it_does: Integrates Medicare and Medicaid to provide comprehensive medical and social services in community settings. Oklahoma has four PACE centers delivering holistic care through interdisciplinary teams.
programs.who_its_for: Individuals 55+ in service areas needing nursing home-level care but able to live safely in the community.
programs.benefit_value: Full coverage of primary, acute, long-term care, and supportive services at no additional cost for dually eligible.

programs.name: Medicare Savings Programs (QMB, SLMB, QI)
programs.what_it_does: Pays Medicare premiums, deductibles, and copayments for low-income beneficiaries. Known as Medicare Savings Program in Oklahoma, including Qualified Medicare Beneficiary (QMB), Specified Low-Income Medicare Beneficiary (SLMB), and Qualifying Individual (QI).
programs.who_its_for: Medicare enrollees with limited income and resources (e.g., QMB up to 100% FPL).
programs.benefit_value: QMB covers Part A/B premiums/costs; SLMB/QI cover Part B premiums up to $40.90/month (2026 rates).

programs.name: Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP)
programs.what_it_does: Provides monthly benefits via EBT card to purchase food at approved retailers. Federal program administered by Oklahoma DHS for eligible seniors.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors 60+ or households meeting income limits (e.g., 130-200% FPL with deductions).

programs.name: Low-Income Home Energy Assistance Program (LIHEAP)
programs.what_it_does: Offers grants to help pay heating/cooling bills and crisis assistance. Administered statewide for low-income households including seniors.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income households (at or below 150% FPL) with seniors, disabled, or children.

programs.name: Weatherization Assistance Program (WAP)
programs.what_it_does: Provides free home energy efficiency improvements like insulation and sealing. Federal program run through Oklahoma agencies targeting vulnerable residents.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income households (200% FPL or below) with elderly or disabled members prioritized.
programs.benefit_value: Free upgrades reducing energy bills by 20-30% long-term.

programs.name: State Health Insurance Assistance Program (SHIP)
programs.what_it_does: Offers free counseling on Medicare options, appeals, and fraud prevention. Administered by Oklahoma Insurance Department as Medicare Assistance Program.
programs.who_its_for: Oklahoma Medicare beneficiaries 65+, caregivers, and families seeking guidance.
programs.benefit_value: Free one-on-one counseling; toll-free help at 800-763-2828.

programs.name: Home-Delivered Meals (Meals on Wheels)
programs.what_it_does: Delivers nutritious meals to homebound seniors to promote independence. Provided through Area Agencies on Aging under Older Americans Act Title III.
programs.who_its_for: Homebound seniors 60+ unable to prepare meals, often low-income or disabled.
programs.benefit_value: 5-7 meals/week meeting 1/3 daily nutrition requirements; suggested donation.

programs.name: Respite Voucher Program
programs.what_it_does: Provides vouchers for caregivers to pay respite providers for short-term relief. Funded under Title III caregiver support through Area Agencies on Aging.
programs.who_its_for: Family caregivers of seniors 60+ or disabled adults needing breaks.
programs.benefit_value: Vouchers up to $20-25/hour for respite services.

programs.name: Senior Community Service Employment Program (SCSEP)
programs.what_it_does: Offers part-time paid training and employment in community service roles. Federal program available through Oklahoma aging services.
programs.who_its_for: Unemployed low-income seniors 55+ seeking work experience.
programs.benefit_value: Minimum wage part-time work (20 hrs/week) with training.

programs.name: Legal Assistance for Seniors (Legal Aid Services of Oklahoma)
programs.what_it_does: Provides free legal help for issues like housing, benefits, and abuse. Delivered via Area Agencies on Aging partnerships under Title III.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors 60+ facing civil legal problems.
programs.benefit_value: Free consultations and representation valued at $200+/hour.

programs.name: Long-Term Care Ombudsman Program
programs.what_it_does: Advocates for residents in nursing homes and assisted living against abuse/neglect. Operated statewide through Area Agencies on Aging.
programs.who_its_for: Seniors in long-term care facilities and their families.
programs.benefit_value: Free advocacy resolving 80%+ of complaints without litigation.

programs.name: ADvantage Waiver Program
programs.what_it_does: Provides Home and Community-Based Services (HCBS) to prevent nursing home placement for elderly or disabled individuals. Services include personal care, assisted living, adult day health care, home modifications, and meals.[2]
programs.who_its_for: Oklahoma residents aged 65+ or disabled adults 21-64 at risk of institutionalization; excludes those with developmental disabilities involving cognitive impairment.[2]
programs.whats_unique: Oklahoma's state Medicaid waiver program offering a specific package of long-term HCBS tailored to state needs, transitioning nursing home residents back home.[2]
programs.benefit_value: Varies by need; includes in-home care, modifications like ramps, and assisted living services without fixed dollar amount.[2]

programs.name: Oklahoma Chore Services (Older Americans Act Title III)
programs.what_it_does: Offers chore services like heavy housework, yard work, and sidewalk maintenance to help seniors stay in their homes. Provides up to $150 annually for materials and supplies.[3]
programs.who_its_for: Oklahomans aged 60+ with difficulty in Instrumental Activities of Daily Living, prioritizing low-income, rural, minority, or disabled individuals.[3]
programs.whats_unique: State-administered OAA program with specific $150 annual materials allowance for chores, targeted via Area Agencies on Aging.[3]
programs.benefit_value: Up to $150 per year for chore materials; service hours based on assessed needs.[3]

programs.name: Senior Farmers’ Market Nutrition Program (SFMNP)
programs.what_it_does: Provides financial support to buy fresh produce from local Oklahoma farmers’ markets. Helps low-income seniors access nutritious foods.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors eligible through state programs, typically 60+.[1]
programs.whats_unique: Oklahoma-specific implementation of federal SFMNP focused on state farmers’ markets for direct produce purchase vouchers.[1]
programs.benefit_value: Vouchers for fresh produce; exact amount varies by state allocation (federal base program).[1]
